campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investment accounts performed and the overall treasurer's report. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to help residents get around using different methods like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment account performance and transactions
City's overall financial health and treasurer's report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving buses, biking, and pedestrian options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to gather community input on a new city rule. After hearing from residents, the council introduced an ordinance (new regulation) and decided to skip the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council plans to vote on officially adopting this ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Fast-track approval process being used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve meeting minutes from their March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on housekeeping items and moving forward with a construction project, though specific details about the project weren't fully outlined in the available agenda information.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes (multiple sessions)
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contracts

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 7, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes and discuss three main projects. The council approved a state grant for the Mexican Heritage Plaza's School of Arts and Culture, extended a contract with a traffic engineering firm for improvements at Bascom Avenue, and awarded a contract for repairing streets and replacing pavement across the city.
keyTopics: State funding approved for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts and culture programs
Traffic signal improvements coming to Bascom Avenue
City streets pavement repair and replacement project awarded

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online. Residents who wanted to speak during public comment could join via Zoom video conference or phone call and use the raise hand feature to be recognized by the chair.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Hybrid in-person and online meeting format

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council opened with the traditional flag salute and designated special time to honor these two important community observances.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular city council session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss progress on a plan to make it easier and safer for people to walk and bike around town. The council also recognized April as both Donate Life Month and Fair Housing Month with official proclamations. Residents had the opportunity to speak to the council about any local issues on their minds.
keyTopics: Active Transportation Plan Phase 2 update - improvements for walking and biking
Changes to how projects will be scored and evaluated
Donate Life Month recognition
Fair Housing Month recognition
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council is holding meetings in a hybrid format, meaning you can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is making it easy for residents to participate and share their opinions, whether you prefer to show up in council chambers or join from home.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or via Zoom
Remote participation options available by phone and video
Written public comment submission through eComment system
Meeting access details and contact information provided

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and new work requests from council members. The council also discussed potential changes to how council vacancies are filled and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules and agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for 2025-2030
New work requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to council vacancy procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved reviewing financial plans for the gas utility and potentially adjusting the rates that residents and businesses pay for natural gas service.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget approval for fiscal year 2027
Potential rate changes for residential gas service (G-1 schedule)
Rate adjustments for commercial and large business gas service (G-2 and G-3 schedules)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund money
